data.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility) — coaching note for S1 `hoot-glove-foyer`, the materialization primitive.

Affirmation: the de-risking order is exactly right for an external-capability story — S1 (the lock-free `read-tree`+`checkout-index` materialization) is front-loaded as a thin vertical slice that proves the primitive before the horizontal sweep, which functions as the SPIKE the rubric wants. The rejected alternative (`git archive`) is named explicitly. Good.

One grounded fidelity gap to pin down before committing the full plan: the EXISTING snapshot path (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/snapshot.py`) uses `git archive`, whose docstring (lines 23–27) documents archive-NATIVE behaviors that `read-tree`+`checkout-index` do NOT replicate: `.gitattributes` `export-ignore` paths are omitted and `export-subst` is applied by `git archive`, but `git checkout-index` honors NEITHER (those are archive-only attributes). The plan asserts a "faithful" materialization but does not state how the chosen `read-tree`+`checkout-index` primitive reproduces (or intentionally diverges from) the archive's `export-ignore`/`export-subst`/submodule/LFS-pointer semantics already documented for the path it replaces.

- [ ] S1: State whether `read-tree`+`checkout-index` reproduces or intentionally diverges from `git archive`'s `export-ignore`/`export-subst` (and submodule/LFS-pointer) semantics documented in `snapshot.py:23-27`; if it diverges, name the fidelity contract the gates rely on.
